playbook_translation_pending: This playbook hasn't been translated yet — showing the English version.
hero_tagline: Building AI tools to augment humanity
bio_p1: Born in Japan, raised in the U.S. I've lived across Spain, Estonia, and beyond. This global perspective shapes everything I build.
bio_p2: After studying Environmental Engineering at Brown, I discovered my real passion: making powerful tools accessible to everyone.
bio_p3: I believe AI should be humanity's great equalizer: the tutors, advisors, and assistants once reserved for the privileged few should be available to all.
bio_quote: AI as the glider for everyone's mind.
consulting_subheading: I help non-technical founders and executives use AI that actually works — not AI that looks impressive in a demo.
consulting_service_strategy_desc: One call. I find the three processes draining your week and show you exactly what to automate first.
consulting_service_build_desc: A week embedded with your team, shipping one real automation end-to-end. You keep the code, the playbook, and the confidence to ship the next one.
consulting_service_training_desc: Monthly check-ins on tooling, agent architecture, and stack choices. For founders and executives who want a brain on call, not a slide deck.
tools_subheading: One-off utilities and side projects I've built. Feel free to use them. Self-guided automated workflows are coming next — drop in your inputs, get the playbook back.
tools_workflows_desc: Drop-in automations you can run yourself — onboarding sequences, research loops, content pipelines. Posting the first ones soon.
faq_subheading: Questions I get asked more than I'd like.
faq_corporate_joke: If this site isn't serious enough for you,
faq_ai_q: Will AI take over the world?
faq_ai_a: No. But I do worry about how it will either force us to sharpen our critical thinking or make us complacent. In many ways, it's like Up or Brave New World. The question isn't whether AI will control us, but whether we'll choose comfort over growth.
faq_japan_q: Is Japan perfect? Do you watch anime?
faq_japan_a: No, Japan is not a perfect country without flaws. And yes, I watch anime. Cowboy Bebop and Paprika are masterpieces. I will die on this hill.
faq_tea_a: Tea. Green tea specifically. And I'm willing to change your mind on this; most green tea in the US is awful (cough cough Lipton). Try the real stuff and we'll talk.
faq_engineering_a: Yes. My advisor once said, "Now that you have a degree, I'm honestly surprised you became an engineer." I also did conservation work in Maui helping maintain Waikamoi Preserve (one of the wettest places in the world). Somehow it stayed that way even with my dry humor.
faq_weird_q: Tell me something weird about you
faq_weird_a:
• I can snap on any surface. Tables, walls, my own face. It's a gift.
  • I beat Total War: Shogun 2 on Legendary as the Tsu faction (the independent republic). If you know, you know.
  • I send postcards to friends around the world. DM me your address, I'm serious.
  • My dream is to make enough friends globally that I always have a couch to crash on.
  • I can only count to three the German way. My hands physically refuse to do it any other way.
faq_funny_a: I grew up in the US of A. I don't have a sense of humour. Please don't sue me.
faq_hill_q: What's a hill you will die on?
faq_hill_a: Cowboy Bebop and Paprika are masterpieces, no debate. Also, if you're anywhere from your late teens through your early thirties, you absolutely must read The Brothers Karamazov. It will change how you see the world.
kaiwa_description: Practice languages through bite-sized AI conversations. Real-time translations, social streaks, and 30-second daily sessions.
footer_tagline: Reluctant technologist. Writing occasional essays on AI, automation, and building alone.
footer_joke: if this site isn't serious enough, visit the corporate version
closing_cta_body: If you're building with AI and want another brain on it, let's talk. Or subscribe below and I'll send the next essay when it's ready.
site_meta_title: Hiro Kuwana — AI Entrepreneur & Founder | Democratizing Education Through Technology
site_meta_description: Hiro Kuwana is a tech entrepreneur building AI solutions to democratize education and empower human potential. Founder of Kaiwa (AI language learning). Making premium tools accessible to everyone.
site_meta_keywords: Hiro Kuwana, AI entrepreneur, startup founder, educational technology, AI democratization, language learning app, EdTech, solo founder, AI automation, reluctant technologist, non-technical to technical, free ICS validator, VCF splitter
now_meta_description: What Hiro Kuwana is currently working on, writing about, reading, and where he is.
now_building_html: Kaiwa — AI-powered language learning through bite-sized conversations. 1,000+ monthly signups, currently focused on the free → paid conversion loop (see my essay Your Funnel Is a Lie).
now_writing_html: Automation playbooks for non-technical founders and executives. How solo founders can run the work of a team with AI. The gap between "AI that looks impressive in a demo" and "AI that actually works."
now_advising_html: A small number of non-technical founders and executives on their AI workflow audits. Booking for April 2026 onward.
now_reading_html: Currently: The Brothers Karamazov (re-read), plus a steady diet of Paul Graham essays and the PostHog + Anthropic blogs.
now_footer_html: Inspired by Derek Sivers' /now movement. Updated roughly monthly.
privacy_meta_description: What hirokuwana.com captures, how to opt out, and how to request deletion.
privacy_short_body: I capture anonymous page-view and click data via PostHog (routed through hirokuwana.com/ingest, not a third-party domain). No account, no cookie banner, no cross-site tracking. If your browser sends a Do Not Track signal, PostHog is not initialized. You can see exactly what's sent by opening DevTools → Network.
privacy_collect_html:
• Page URL and anonymous session ID (for funnels and retention analysis)
  • Referrer (how you arrived)
  • Device category (desktop, mobile, tablet) and viewport size
  • Country (never city, never IP stored)
  • Clicks on outbound links, essay reads, and CTA buttons
privacy_exclude_html:
• Your name, email, or any identifying information unless you explicitly submit it (e.g., subscribing to a newsletter)
  • Form-input keystrokes — all input fields are masked in any session recordings
  • Data from users with Do Not Track enabled
  • Anything on the ICS Validator or VCF Splitter pages beyond the page view — file processing is 100% in your browser and nothing is ever uploaded
privacy_third_html:
• PostHog — product analytics, US hosting
  • Vercel — hosting provider, collects standard web-server logs
  • Google Fonts — serves the Inter typeface (no cookies)
privacy_requests_html: Want your data removed? Want to opt out proactively? Email hiro@trykaiwa.com and I'll purge any associated session data from PostHog.
